task_scores.ships_wrong_risk: If @react-pdf/renderer version or Next 16 compatibility is wrong, the entire PDF pipeline fails and Phase 2 blocks.
task_scores.reasoning: Essential foundation with exact paths and verification; no direct user-visible output yet but unlocks all downstream work.

task_scores.ships_wrong_risk: If Content-Length trust assumption is violated (client lies), request.json() buffers unbounded body and OOM kills the server; reverse-proxy body limit is only documented, not implemented.
task_scores.reasoning: Core backend; refined plan adds HTTP status assertions and documents body-limit limitation; validation paths are explicit and testable.

task_scores.task_title: Phase 3 — Client Integration and Download Flow
task_scores.ships_wrong_risk: If getApiBase() returns wrong value when basePath is configured, fetch hits 404 in production and Download PDF silently fails.
task_scores.reasoning: Primary user flow; orphaned toPng promise fix and toWelderName invariant reduce runtime errors; chart fallback is well specified.

task_scores.ships_wrong_risk: If Step 4.4 grep checks for width/height mismatch (e.g. inline style vs computed), test passes but PDF capture dimensions are wrong.
task_scores.higher_leverage_alternative: Assert computed style or getBoundingClientRect instead of style attribute for 600×200; optional but more robust.
task_scores.reasoning: Refined plan adds dimension assertion, toWelderName invariant, CI_LOW_MEM skip, and fetch restore in afterEach; meets .cursorrules verification requirement.

critical_issues: Step 2.1: Server does not enforce read limit at stream level — client can lie about Content-Length and cause OOM; mitigation is documentation only, not code.
Step 4.4: Grep on style attribute (width.*600) may pass if CSS uses different units or computed values; programmatic assertion would be safer but is marked optional in Known Issues.
minor_issues: CI_LOW_MEM requires CI=true; CI=1 (common in CI systems) will not trigger skip — 413 test may OOM in some environments.
sanitizeText file path clarification (WelderReportPDF.tsx) is correct but was a refinement fix; no regression risk.
suggestions: Add Pre-Flight curl step to CI or release checklist for basePath when NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH is set — catches getApiBase bugs before deploy.
Consider asserting chartEl.offsetWidth === 600 and chartEl.offsetHeight === 200 in Step 4.4 as a stronger invariant than style string match.
Document CI env format (CI=true vs CI=1) in Known Issues so teams know when 413 skip applies.
dependency_order: Phase 1 before Phase 2 — WelderReportPDF required. Phase 2 before Phase 3 — API route required for fetch. Phase 3 before Phase 4 — handleDownloadPDF and mocks required for tests. Phase 4 Step 4.6 depends on Step 2.1 route.
premortem_top_risk: html-to-image fails on Recharts in target browsers, chartDataUrl is null, and PDF ships without chart; user expectation mismatch or perceived broken feature.
goal_alignment_notes: All four phases serve the stated goal: supervisors and welders download professional PDFs from /seagull/welder/[id]. Refined fixes (HTTP assertions, toWelderName invariant, chart dimensions, Content-Length doc) close adversarial gaps. Visible output within 48h is achievable if phases 1–3 ship first; Phase 4 adds confidence.
